---
title: "Heritability & Genetic Correlations"
subtitle: ""
author: "ESSGN 2026 Workshop"
date: 2026-06-01
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    center: false
    chalkboard: false
    smaller: false
    scrollable: false
    transition: fade
    fig-align: center
    html-math-method: mathjax
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.1
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Part 0: Statistical Foundations {background-color="#8e44ad" style="color: white;"}

## Random variables and expectation

A **random variable** $X$ is a quantity whose value is uncertain. It has a distribution.

The **expectation** (mean) summarizes its center:

$$E[X] = \mu_X$$

A linear transformation of a random variable $aX + b$ has expectation:

$$E[aX + b] = a\,E[X] + b$$

## Expectation of a sum of random variables

The expectation of a **sum** of random variables is the sum of their individual expectations - **linearity of expectation**:

$$E[aX + bY] = a\,E[X] + b\,E[Y]$$

$$
E\left[\sum_{i=1}^n a_i X_i + c\right]
= \sum_{i=1}^n a_i\,E[X_i] + c
$$
